Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-03-11T09:38:07.1580338Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:36:34.70782973Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":56961,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Read-only models with @@access(Access.public) generate incomplete code requiring  workaround\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen a TypeSpec read-only model has `@@access(Access.public, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;)` applied, the management plane generator (`http-client-csharp-mgmt`) produces incomplete/incorrect generated code, requiring SDK authors to use `` in the `.csproj` and provide full custom replacements for both the model and its serialization.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\nFound during the `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` migration to TypeSpec ([PR #56586](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56586)).\\n\\nThe `ExtensionProfile` model is a read-only model (only has getter properties). Despite having `@@access(Access.public, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;)` in `client.tsp`, the generated code is incomplete — requiring the following workaround in the `.csproj`:\\n\\n```xml\\n\\n\\n```\\n\\nPlus two custom replacement files:\\n- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs` — constructors, properties, raw data field\\n- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs` — full `IJsonModel` / `IPersistableModel` implementation\\n\\nThe model factory (`ArmHciModelFactory`) generates a public method that takes `ExtensionProfile` as a parameter, which causes **CS0051** (inconsistent accessibility) if the generator makes the model `internal`.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nWhen `@@access(Access.public, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;)` is set on a read-only model:\\n\\n1. The generator should produce the **complete** model class (public, with constructors, properties, and the raw data field)\\n2. The generator should produce the **complete** serialization implementation\\n3. No `` workaround should be needed\\n4. The model factory should be able to reference the model without accessibility issues\\n\\n## Actual Behavior\\n\\nThe generator\\u0026#39;s usage analysis overrides or interferes with the `@@access` decorator for read-only models, resulting in incomplete generated code that cannot be used directly.\\n\\n## Affected Files\\n\\n- **Generator**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n- **Affected SDK**: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/`\\n- **Workaround files**:\\n  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs`\\n  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs`\\n  - `.csproj` `` entries\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nAny TypeSpec-based management SDK migration that has read-only models exposed via model factory methods may hit this issue. SDK authors must manually maintain custom model + serialization files, which is error-prone and creates maintenance burden.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56961\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-11T09:34:19Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-11T09:34:19Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-11T09:38:07.1615455Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:36:42.598693093Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4057038402,\"number\":56961,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Read-only models with @@access(Access.public) generate incomplete code requiring \\u003cCompile Remove\\u003e workaround\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen a TypeSpec read-only model has `@@access(Access.public, \\\"csharp\\\")` applied, the management plane generator (`http-client-csharp-mgmt`) produces incomplete/incorrect generated code, requiring SDK authors to use `\\u003cCompile Remove\\u003e` in the `.csproj` and provide full custom replacements for both the model and its serialization.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\nFound during the `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` migration to TypeSpec ([PR #56586](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56586)).\\n\\nThe `ExtensionProfile` model is a read-only model (only has getter properties). Despite having `@@access(Access.public, \\\"csharp\\\")` in `client.tsp`, the generated code is incomplete — requiring the following workaround in the `.csproj`:\\n\\n```xml\\n\\u003cCompile Remove=\\\"Generated\\\\Models\\\\ExtensionProfile.cs\\\" /\\u003e\\n\\u003cCompile Remove=\\\"Generated\\\\Models\\\\ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs\\\" /\\u003e\\n```\\n\\nPlus two custom replacement files:\\n- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs` — constructors, properties, raw data field\\n- `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs` — full `IJsonModel\\u003cT\\u003e` / `IPersistableModel\\u003cT\\u003e` implementation\\n\\nThe model factory (`ArmHciModelFactory`) generates a public method that takes `ExtensionProfile` as a parameter, which causes **CS0051** (inconsistent accessibility) if the generator makes the model `internal`.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nWhen `@@access(Access.public, \\\"csharp\\\")` is set on a read-only model:\\n\\n1. The generator should produce the **complete** model class (public, with constructors, properties, and the raw data field)\\n2. The generator should produce the **complete** serialization implementation\\n3. No `\\u003cCompile Remove\\u003e` workaround should be needed\\n4. The model factory should be able to reference the model without accessibility issues\\n\\n## Actual Behavior\\n\\nThe generator's usage analysis overrides or interferes with the `@@access` decorator for read-only models, resulting in incomplete generated code that cannot be used directly.\\n\\n## Affected Files\\n\\n- **Generator**: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n- **Affected SDK**: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/`\\n- **Workaround files**:\\n  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs`\\n  - `src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs`\\n  - `.csproj` `\\u003cCompile Remove\\u003e` entries\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nAny TypeSpec-based management SDK migration that has read-only models exposed via model factory methods may hit this issue. SDK authors must manually maintain custom model + serialization files, which is error-prone and creates maintenance burden.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjUxOTYxMzk=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178448111,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt\",\"name\":\"Mgmt\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This 2026-03-11T09:38:07.1641320Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:37:09.488005996Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":3,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}}
2026-03-11T09:38:07.1658851Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:37:36.310389278Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a bug in the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` management plane generator where read-only TypeSpec models annotated with `@@access(Access.public, \"csharp\")` produce incomplete generated code. The generator's internal usage analysis overrides the explicit `@@access` decorator, causing accessibility conflicts (CS0051) with the model factory and forcing SDK authors to use `\u003cCompile Remove\u003e` workarounds with full hand-written replacements.\n\nThe issue was filed by a MEMBER and first surfaced during the `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` TypeSpec migration (PR #56586).\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe generator's usage/reachability analysis appears to classify read-only models (models with only getter properties) as `internal` based on how they are consumed, even when `@@access(Access.public)` is explicitly set. This analysis takes precedence over the TypeSpec decorator, so the generated `.cs` files are incomplete (likely missing the full public class body or serialization wiring). Because the model factory method is emitted as `public` (correctly referencing the public model), a CS0051 \"inconsistent accessibility\" error results.\n\nKey files to investigate:\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` — look for usage analysis / accessibility resolution logic\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/` — check if any visitor downgrades access based on read-only detection\n- The `@@access` decorator handling in the emitter: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/emitter/src/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🐞 Reproduction Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. Define a TypeSpec model with only read-only (getter) properties and annotate it with `@@access(Access.public, \"csharp\")` in `client.tsp`\n2. Run the mgmt generator (`/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1`)\n3. Observe that the generated `Models/\u003cModelName\u003e.cs` and `Models/\u003cModelName\u003e.Serialization.cs` are incomplete\n4. Observe CS0051 build error in the model factory file referencing the model\n\nA minimal repro could be extracted from the `ExtensionProfile` model in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Fix Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Identify where the generator resolves effective accessibility for model types\n- [ ] Ensure that an explicit `@@access(Access.public)` decorator on a model always wins over heuristic/usage-based analysis\n- [ ] Verify that read-only models (getter-only properties) are not incorrectly downgraded to `internal`\n- [ ] Confirm the model factory generator reads the same resolved accessibility when emitting parameter types\n- [ ] Add a test case: read-only TypeSpec model + `@@access(Access.public)` → generated code is complete and public\n- [ ] After the fix, remove the `\u003cCompile Remove\u003e` workaround and custom files from `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci`\n- [ ] Run `Generate.ps1` and verify the Hci SDK builds cleanly with no CS0051 errors\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📎 Relevant Links\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- PR where workaround was introduced: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56586\n- Generator source: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\n- Workaround files: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs`, `ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs`\n- TypeSpec `@access` decorator docs: https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/libraries/azure-client-generator-core/reference/decorators#@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core.access\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e💡 Notes for Reviewers\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Scope:** Any management SDK migration with read-only models surfaced via model factory methods is potentially affected. Worth auditing other recently migrated SDKs.\n- **Owners:** Per CODEOWNERS, the generator is owned by @JoshLove-msft, @m-nash, @jorgerangel-msft, @live1206, @ArcturusZhang.\n- **Priority:** Medium-High — it blocks clean TypeSpec migrations and forces manual maintenance of generated code equivalents.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":56961},"name":"add_comment"}}}
2026-03-11T09:38:07.1673978Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:37:36.315105113Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"error":{"code":-32602,"message":"E007: Comment contains 14 mentions, maximum is 10"},"id":4,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}}
2026-03-11T09:38:07.1690923Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T09:37:46.436930179Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a bug in the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` management plane generator where read-only TypeSpec models annotated with `@@access(Access.public, \"csharp\")` produce incomplete generated code. The generator's internal usage analysis overrides the explicit `@@access` decorator, causing accessibility conflicts (CS0051) with the model factory and forcing SDK authors to use `\u003cCompile Remove\u003e` workarounds with full hand-written replacements.\n\nThe issue was filed by a MEMBER and first surfaced during the `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci` TypeSpec migration (PR #56586).\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe generator's usage/reachability analysis appears to classify read-only models (models with only getter properties) as `internal` based on how they are consumed, even when `@@access(Access.public)` is explicitly set. This analysis takes precedence over the TypeSpec decorator, so the generated `.cs` files are incomplete. Because the model factory method is emitted as `public` (correctly referencing the public model), a CS0051 \"inconsistent accessibility\" error results.\n\nKey files to investigate:\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/` — look for usage analysis / accessibility resolution logic\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/` — check if any visitor downgrades access based on read-only detection\n- The `@@access` decorator handling in the emitter: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/emitter/src/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🐞 Reproduction Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. Define a TypeSpec model with only read-only (getter) properties and annotate it with `@@access(Access.public, \"csharp\")` in `client.tsp`\n2. Run the mgmt generator (`/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1`)\n3. Observe that the generated `Models/\u003cModelName\u003e.cs` and `Models/\u003cModelName\u003e.Serialization.cs` are incomplete\n4. Observe CS0051 build error in the model factory file referencing the model\n\nA minimal repro can be extracted from the `ExtensionProfile` model in `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Fix Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Identify where the generator resolves effective accessibility for model types\n- [ ] Ensure that an explicit `@@access(Access.public)` decorator always wins over heuristic/usage-based analysis\n- [ ] Verify that read-only models (getter-only properties) are not incorrectly downgraded to `internal`\n- [ ] Confirm the model factory generator reads the same resolved accessibility when emitting parameter types\n- [ ] Add a test case: read-only TypeSpec model + `@@access(Access.public)` → generated code is complete and public\n- [ ] After the fix, remove the `\u003cCompile Remove\u003e` workaround and custom files from `Azure.ResourceManager.Hci`\n- [ ] Run `Generate.ps1` and verify the Hci SDK builds cleanly with no CS0051 errors\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📎 Relevant Links\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- PR where workaround was introduced: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56586\n- Generator source: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\n- Workaround files: `sdk/azurestackhci/Azure.ResourceManager.Hci/src/Custom/ExtensionProfile.cs`, `ExtensionProfile.Serialization.cs`\n- TypeSpec `@access` decorator docs: https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/libraries/azure-client-generator-core/reference/decorators#@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core.access\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e💡 Notes for Reviewers\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Scope:** Any management SDK migration with read-only models surfaced via model factory methods is potentially affected. Worth auditing other recently migrated SDKs.\n- **Owners:** Per CODEOWNERS, the generator is owned by the http-client-csharp-mgmt team.\n- **Priority:** Medium-High — it blocks clean TypeSpec migrations and forces manual maintenance of generated code equivalents.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":56961},"name":"add_comment"}}}
